agent.root_goal: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.
agent.task: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.

action.reasoning: The task of building a full-stack todo app is complex and naturally decomposes into four distinct phases: schema design, backend implementation, frontend implementation, and integration testing. These phases have clear dependencies: the backend depends on the schema, the frontend depends on the backend API, and the tests depend on both. I will spawn subtasks for each phase to allow for parallel work where possible (e.g., schema design can start immediately, backend can start once schema is done, etc.) and ensure each subtask has a clear goal and output.

subtasks.goal: Design the database schema for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask defines the data model for todos, including fields like id, title, completed status, and timestamps. It serves as the foundation for the backend API and ensures data consistency across the application.

outputs.description: JSON or SQL definition of the todo database schema.

subtasks.goal: Implement the backend API for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask builds the server-side logic, including CRUD endpoints for todos, using the schema defined in root.1. It provides the API that the frontend will consume.

outputs.description: Source code for the backend API, including server setup, routes, and database interactions.
Documentation of the API endpoints, methods, request/response formats.

subtasks.goal: Implement the frontend for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask creates the user interface, including components for listing, adding, editing, and deleting todos. It interacts with the backend API defined in root.2.

outputs.description: Source code for the frontend application, including components, state management, and API integration.

subtasks.goal: Write integration tests for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask creates tests that verify the interaction between the frontend and backend, ensuring the full-stack application works correctly. It depends on both the backend and frontend implementations.

outputs.description: Source code for integration tests, including test cases for CRUD operations and UI interactions.

self_role.goal: Integrate all components and verify the full-stack application works end-to-end.

outputs.description: A summary report of the full-stack todo app, including links to all components, test results, and any known issues.

self_role.condition: done("root.1/schema") AND done("root.2/backend_code") AND done("root.3/frontend_code") AND done("root.4/integration_tests")

action.reasoning: The task of designing a database schema for a todo app is a focused, self-contained design exercise. It does not require complex decomposition or parallel subtasks, as the schema definition is a single artifact. I can competently produce a standard SQL schema definition that includes necessary fields (id, title, completed, timestamps) and constraints in a single execution step.

result_outputs.description: SQL definition of the todo database schema including id, title, completed status, and timestamps.

action.reasoning: The task is to implement a backend API based on a provided schema. This is a standard, well-defined programming task that can be completed in a single focused effort. The schema is available in shared memory, so no data is missing. Decomposing this into subtasks (e.g., one for routes, one for DB connection) would add unnecessary overhead and coordination complexity for a relatively small codebase. Therefore, I will execute the task myself, producing both the backend code and the API specification.

result_outputs.description: Source code for the backend API, including server setup, routes, and database interactions.
Documentation of the API endpoints, methods, request/response formats.

action.reasoning: The task is to write integration tests for the full-stack todo app. I have access to the backend code (Express/PostgreSQL) and frontend code (React). Integration tests should verify the API endpoints and potentially the full stack interaction. Since I have all the necessary context (backend and frontend code), I can execute this task directly without needing to spawn subtasks or defer. I will create a comprehensive test suite using Jest and Supertest for the backend API, and React Testing Library for the frontend components, ensuring they work together.

result_outputs.description: Source code for integration tests, including test cases for CRUD operations and UI interactions.
